name: Praline Cookies
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Finely chopped Pecans or Hazelnuts (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F and grease a large baking sheet with butter or margarine.
2. For the praline: In a heavy saucepan, stir together 1/3 cup sugar and water. Cook over medium-high heat until boiling, then cook for 2-1/2 to 3-1/2 minutes more or until syrup is deep golden brown. Remove from heat, stir in nuts, and immediately pour onto the prepared baking sheet.
3. Allow praline mixture to cool completely on a wire rack until firm.
4. Transfer cooled praline to a heavy plastic bag and crush into small pieces using a rolling pin.
5. For cookies: In a large mixing bowl, beat margarine or butter with an electric mixer until softened (about 30 seconds). Add half of the flour, all of the brown sugar, 1/2 cup granulated sugar, eggs, vanilla extract, and baking soda. Beat thoroughly, scraping sides of the bowl occasionally.
6. Mix in remaining flour and crushed praline. Drop 1/4 cup of dough at a time onto ungreased cookie sheets, spacing them about 4 inches apart.
7. Flatten each cookie to about 1 inch thick and bake for 13-15 minutes or until edges are lightly browned.
8. Remove cookies from the oven and let cool on the baking sheets for 1 minute before transferring to wire racks to finish cooling.
9. If desired, melt chocolate and drizzle over cooled cookies, sprinkling with chopped pecans or hazelnuts (optional).
